title: US x Iran permanent peace deal by...?
description: Date-variant market on whether the US and Iran agree to a permanent peace deal.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if the US and Iran agree to a permanent peace deal explicitly ending hostilities, signed or confirmed by both governments, by the target date. Temporary ceasefires or non-definitive progress do not count.
snapshot_note: Reused from v2. Specific outcome page still needs cleaner rule capture before formal promotion.

title: Strait of Hormuz traffic returns to normal by end of June?
description: Binary market on whether Strait of Hormuz traffic normalizes by the end of June 2026.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if IMF Portwatch publishes a 7-day moving average of transit calls, Arrivals of Ships, for the Strait of Hormuz equal to or above 60 for any date between market creation and June 30, 2026. Daily transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch are not considered. Resolution source is IMF Portwatch Strait of Hormuz transit calls data.
snapshot_note: Reused from v2. Objective traffic market is adjacent to broader peace/deal-package claims.

title: Claude 5 released by...?
description: Date-bucket market family on whether Anthropic Claude 5 is publicly released by a target date.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if Anthropic's Claude 5 model is made available to the general public by the target date. Claude 5 must be launched and publicly accessible, including open beta or open rolling waitlist signups; closed beta or private access does not suffice. Claude 5 refers to a product explicitly named Claude 5 or one recognized as a successor to Claude 4. Products labeled Claude 4.5 or similar do not count. Primary resolution source is official Anthropic information, with additional verification from consensus credible reporting.
snapshot_note: Reused from v2. Indirect for Claude 4.8 / Google Vertex availability claims.

title: Amazon 2026 capex above ___?
description: Threshold market family on Amazon 2026 capital expenditures.
resolution_rules: Resolves Yes if Amazon's Purchases of Property and Equipment, capital expenditures, for full-year 2026 as reported in official company earnings materials for Q4 2026 is above the listed amount. Uses the most numerically precise version of the metric in official earnings materials, including press releases, investor presentations, regulatory filings, and earnings webcast materials if needed. Alternate metrics that differ in definition or scope are not considered.
snapshot_note: Reused from v2. Weak proxy for aggregate AI spend, model-pricing, and enterprise-cost thesis.
